chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: Analyse the political causes of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Political causes included the annexation of Indian states by the British through policies like the Doctrine of Lapse and the removal of traditional rulers, which created widespread resentment among Indian princes and nobles.

questions.question: Examine the economic causes that contributed to the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Heavy taxation, destruction of traditional industries and exploitation of farmers created severe economic hardship, leading many people to support the revolt.

questions.question: Discuss the military causes of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Indian soldiers faced discrimination, lower salaries and the controversial greased cartridges, which triggered the revolt among sepoys.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the greased cartridge issue in starting the revolt.
questions.answer: The introduction of greased cartridges offended the religious beliefs of Hindu and Muslim soldiers, creating anger and distrust towards British authorities.

questions.question: Analyse the role of sepoys in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Sepoys initiated the revolt by rebelling against their British officers, which encouraged civilians and rulers to join the uprising.

questions.question: Evaluate the role of Bahadur Shah Zafar in the revolt.
questions.answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar became the symbolic leader of the revolt in Delhi, giving legitimacy and unity to the rebellion.

questions.question: Examine the contribution of Rani Lakshmibai in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Rani Lakshmibai led the resistance in Jhansi and fought bravely against the British army, becoming one of the most important leaders of the revolt.

questions.question: Discuss the role of Nana Sahib in the revolt.
questions.answer: Nana Sahib led the revolt in Kanpur after the British refused to recognise his claim to his father's pension.

questions.question: Analyse the spread of the Revolt of 1857 across northern India.
questions.answer: The revolt spread quickly from Meerut to Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow, Jhansi and other regions due to widespread dissatisfaction with British rule.

questions.question: Explain why Delhi became the centre of the revolt.
questions.answer: Delhi became the centre because rebels declared Bahadur Shah Zafar as their leader and it held symbolic importance as the Mughal capital.

questions.question: Discuss the role of peasants and civilians in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Peasants and civilians joined the revolt because they were unhappy with high taxes and British economic policies.

questions.question: Analyse the reasons for the failure of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: The revolt failed due to lack of unity, absence of central leadership and the superior military strength of the British.

questions.question: Evaluate the military strength of the British during the revolt.
questions.answer: The British had better weapons, disciplined armies and support from loyal soldiers, which helped them suppress the revolt.

questions.question: Explain the consequences of the Revolt of 1857 on British administration.
questions.answer: After the revolt, the British Crown took direct control of India and ended the rule of the East India Company.

questions.question: Discuss the changes in the British army after the revolt.
questions.answer: The British reorganised the army by increasing the number of European soldiers and reducing the proportion of Indian soldiers.

questions.question: Explain the changes in British policies towards Indian rulers after the revolt.
questions.answer: The British stopped annexing Indian states and promised to respect the rights of Indian princes.

questions.question: Analyse the significance of the Revolt of 1857 in Indian history.
questions.answer: The revolt marked the first large-scale resistance against British rule and laid the foundation for future nationalist movements.

questions.question: Discuss the impact of the revolt on Indian society.
questions.answer: The revolt increased political awareness and strengthened the desire for independence among Indians.

questions.question: Explain how the Revolt of 1857 influenced British governance in India.
questions.answer: The revolt forced the British to introduce administrative reforms and rule India more cautiously.

questions.question: Evaluate the importance of leadership in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Leaders like Rani Lakshmibai and Nana Sahib inspired people to fight against British rule and mobilised resistance.

questions.question: Explain how different groups participated in the revolt.
questions.answer: Sepoys, rulers, peasants and civilians participated in the revolt due to dissatisfaction with British rule.

questions.question: Analyse the importance of regional centres in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Cities like Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow and Jhansi became centres of resistance and organised rebellion.

questions.question: Explain how British policies created dissatisfaction among different groups before the revolt.
questions.answer: Policies affecting rulers, peasants and soldiers created widespread dissatisfaction which led to rebellion.

questions.question: Discuss the historical importance of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: The revolt was a turning point that ended Company rule and led to direct British control of India.

questions.question: Explain why the Revolt of 1857 is considered a turning point in Indian history.
questions.answer: It changed the political structure of India and influenced future resistance movements against colonial rule.
